title: Feature Flag Misconfiguration Exposing Unfinished Feature to All Users
symptoms: Checkout page rendering with broken CSS and missing elements
Payment validation errors for all checkout attempts through new flow
Feature flag dashboard showing 100% rollout instead of 5% canary
Customer support reports of checkout not working
Checkout conversion rate dropping sharply in analytics
root_cause: Feature flag targeting rule was modified from percentage-based (5%) to boolean true for all users during a flag audit that accidentally saved the wrong configuration
resolution: Reverted the feature flag to 5% canary rollout, cleared CDN cache serving the new checkout assets, and verified the original checkout flow was restored for 95% of users
preventive_actions: Implement feature flag change approval workflow for production flags
Add feature flag change alerting for unexpected rollout percentage changes
Create feature flag audit logging with diff comparison
Implement feature flag rollback automation triggered by conversion metrics
Add visual regression testing for feature-flagged UI changes

timeline.event: Feature flag new-checkout-v2 accidentally set to 100% rollout
Checkout conversion rate drops sharply in real-time analytics
Customer support queue begins filling with checkout complaints
Priya Sharma identifies feature flag misconfiguration
Feature flag reverted to 5% canary rollout
CDN cache cleared for checkout assets
Checkout conversion rate recovering, incident resolved

embedding_summary: Feature flag misconfiguration in LaunchDarkly exposed unfinished checkout-v2 to 100% of users instead of 5% canary group. Broken checkout flow caused conversion rate to drop from 12% to 2% for 22 minutes. 3,400 users encountered errors. Resolved by reverting flag to canary rollout.
